name: Cornmeal or Oatmeal Scrapple
recipeInstructions:
1. Put the pork, veal knuckle, onions and 2 tablespoons of the salt into a large pot. Cover with water and simmer for 2 hours or until the meat is very tender.
2. Grind the meat. Discard the veal knuckle and skim fat from the broth. Strain the broth.
3. Return the strained broth to the pot and add the ground pork, remaining salt, pepper, thyme, sage, mace, and cornmeal. Cook slowly for 1 hour, stirring occasionally so it doesn't stick. It should cook until it reaches a mush consistency. Add more hot water if necessary.
4. Pour into greased loaf pans to set and harden. To serve, slice thin and fry in a little oil until crisp brown.
